metadata.title: Republic of Kenya — Complete Government Structure
metadata.description: Full hierarchical tree of all government positions and current officeholders, from the President to the Village Elder level.
metadata.salary_source: SRC Gazette Notices No. 10346, 10348 & 10350 (9 Aug 2023), Third Remuneration & Benefits Review Cycle, Phase II effective 1 July 2024
metadata.parliament_source: Official Kenya Parliament PDF — List of Members by Parties, 13th Parliament, 22 May 2023
notes: All salaries are gross monthly in KSh. Net pay is lower after PAYE, SHIF (2.75%), NSSF, and Housing Levy (1.5%).
County Commissioners, Chiefs and Assistant Chiefs are career civil servants; individual names vary and are not centrally gazetted.
MPs list reflects May 2023 official Parliament PDF; some seats have since changed via by-elections.
A new Fourth SRC Review Cycle (2025-2029) commenced Dec 2025 — some figures may have updated.

tree.position: President of the Republic of Kenya
tree.current_officeholder: H.E. Dr. William Samoei Ruto, C.G.H.
tree.appointment_method: Popular direct election — must win ≥50%+1 votes AND ≥25% in ≥24 of 47 counties (Art. 138)

children.appointment_method: Nominated by President; approved by National Assembly (Art. 149)
children.notes: Sworn in after impeachment of Rigathi Gachagua on 17 Oct 2024
Coordinates the public service; chairs the Advisory Committee of PSC
Manages the President's office and schedule

children.appointment_method: Nominated by President; approved by National Assembly (Art. 156)
children.notes: Principal legal adviser to Government; ex-officio Cabinet member (non-voting)

children.salary_source: SRC Gazette Notice No. 10346 (9 Aug 2023) — applies to each CS
children.appointment_method: Nominated by President; vetted and approved by National Assembly (Art. 152)
children.notes: Reshuffled July 2024, November 2024, January 2025, March–April 2025

children.position: CS — Foreign and Diaspora Affairs (Prime Cabinet Secretary)

children.notes: Line ministry over County Commissioners, Chiefs and Assistant Chiefs

children.current_officeholder: Varies by county — career civil servants appointed by Interior Ministry
children.salary_source: PSC salary scales Job Group Q, Phase II eff. 1 Jul 2024
children.appointment_method: Career appointment through PSC / Ministry of Interior
children.notes: Represents National Govt at county level; chairs County Security Intelligence Committee

children.current_officeholder: Varies by sub-county — career civil servants
children.salary_source: PSC salary scales Job Group P, Phase II eff. 1 Jul 2024
children.appointment_method: Career appointment through PSC / Interior Ministry
children.notes: Represents National Govt at sub-county/constituency level

children.salary_source: PSC/SRC civil service grades, Third Review Cycle 2021/22–2024/25
children.appointment_method: Competitively recruited by PSC — open advertisement; must reside in location; KCSE grade C minimum; NOT elected
children.constitutional_basis: National Government Co-ordination Act, 2013; Chiefs Act (Cap. 128)
children.notes: Most common salary ~KSh 40,000/mo

children.level: Sub-Location — National Govt Administration (Lowest formal tier)
children.salary_source: PSC/SRC civil service grades, Third Review Cycle 2021/22–2024/25
children.appointment_method: Competitively recruited by PSC — open advertisement; must reside in sub-location; KCSE C plain minimum; NOT elected
children.constitutional_basis: National Government Co-ordination Act, 2013; Chiefs Act (Cap. 128)
children.notes: Most common salary ~KSh 30,000/mo; manages Nyumba Kumi units

children.appointment_method: Selected by the community and the Assistant Chief
children.constitutional_basis: National Government Co-ordination Act, 2013 (Nyumba Kumi provisions)
children.notes: May receive token allowances from county budgets in some areas

children.position: CS — National Treasury and Economic Planning
children.notes: Appointed July 2024 broad-based cabinet
Moved from Defence docket in April 2025 reshuffle
Moved from Environment docket in April 2025 reshuffle

children.notes: Moved from Health docket in April 2025 reshuffle
Appointed January 2025

children.position: CS — Lands, Public Works, Housing and Urban Development

children.notes: Appointed July 2024 broad-based cabinet
Former Governor of Nakuru County

children.notes: Former Governor of Kiambu County

children.position: CS — Mining, Blue Economy and Maritime Affairs
children.notes: Former Governor of Mombasa County
Former Governor of Kakamega County

children.position: CS — Youth Affairs, Creative Economy and Sports

children.notes: Former KenGen CEO

children.position: CS — East African Community, ASALs and Regional Development
CS — Gender, Culture, Arts and Heritage
CS — Public Service and Human Capital Development
children.notes: Nominated March 2025; former MP for Mbeere North

children.appointment_method: Recommended by PSC; nominated and appointed by President with NA approval (Art. 155)
children.notes: March 2025 reshuffle appointed 15 new PSs (Executive Order No. 1 of 2025)

children.notes: Members: President, DP, CS Defence, CS Foreign Affairs, CS Interior, CJ (observer), AG (observer)

children.appointment_method: Appointed by President with National Assembly approval (Art. 245)

children.notes: Sworn in October 2025

children.notes: Sworn in October 2025

Appointed by President with National Assembly approval

children.current_officeholder: General Charles Muriu Kahariri, CBS, EBS, ndc
children.appointment_method: Appointed by President with National Assembly approval (Art. 241)

children.notes: Commander-in-Chief is the President

children.appointment_method: Presidential appointment on advice of CDF
Presidential appointment on advice of CDF
Presidential appointment on advice of CDF
children.notes: First woman to command Kenya Navy

children.current_officeholder: Speaker: Dr. Moses M. Wetang'ula, E.G.H.
children.salary_source: SRC Gazette Notice No. 10350 (9 Aug 2023) — Speaker salary
children.appointment_method: Speaker elected by MPs; MPs elected by popular vote (Art. 97, 106)
children.term: Speaker: co-terminus with Parliament; MPs: 5 years
children.notes: 290 constituency seats + 47 County Women Reps + 12 Nominated = 349 total members

children.appointment_method: Popular direct election (constituencies); Presidential nomination (nominated seats)
children.notes: List as at 22 May 2023 (Official Parliament PDF). CWR = County Women Representative.

members.name: Ruku, Geoffrey Kariuki Kiringa
members.notes: Nominated CS for Public Service Mar 2025

members.name: Ng'ongo, John Mbadi
members.notes: Now CS National Treasury — seat likely vacated

children.salary_source: SRC Gazette Notice No. 10350 (9 Aug 2023) — Speaker salary
children.appointment_method: Speaker elected by Senators; 47 elected Senators; 20 nominated women; 2 youth; 2 PWD
children.notes: 67 total senators + Speaker Kingi (non-voting). Former Governor of Kilifi County.
Also Senator for Meru County

children.appointment_method: Popular direct election — one per county (Art. 98)
children.notes: 47 elected + 16 nominated women + 2 youth + 2 PWD = 67 total

children.current_officeholder: Headed by Chief Justice Martha Koome
children.appointment_method: Independent — Judicial Service Commission (JSC) oversees appointments; President appoints on JSC recommendation with NA approval
children.notes: Fully independent of the Executive and Legislature

children.position: Chief Justice and President of the Supreme Court
children.salary_source: SRC Third Remuneration Review Cycle, FY 2024/25 (consolidated pay)
children.appointment_method: JSC competitive public interviews → National Assembly approval → Presidential appointment (Art. 166)
children.term: 10 years or until age 70, whichever is earlier
children.notes: First woman Chief Justice in Kenya's history; also chairs JSC

children.position: Deputy Chief Justice and Deputy President of the Supreme Court
children.salary_source: SRC Third Remuneration Review Cycle, FY 2024/25
children.appointment_method: JSC competitive interviews → NA approval → Presidential appointment (Art. 166)
children.notes: Member of JSC ex-officio

children.appointment_method: JSC recommendation → NA approval → Presidential appointment (Art. 166)
JSC recommendation → NA approval → Presidential appointment
JSC recommendation → NA approval → Presidential appointment
JSC recommendation → NA approval → Presidential appointment
JSC recommendation → NA approval → Presidential appointment
children.notes: Former President of Court of Appeal

children.position: Court of Appeal (~50 Justices of Appeal)
children.appointment_method: JSC competitive interviews → NA approval → Presidential appointment (Art. 166)
children.notes: Hears appeals from High Court; also hears electoral disputes at county level

children.current_officeholder: Principal Judge + Judges deployed across 47 counties
children.notes: Includes specialised divisions: Employment & Labour Relations Court, Environment & Land Court

children.current_officeholder: Chief Magistrates, Senior Principal Magistrates, Principal Magistrates, Senior Resident Magistrates, Resident Magistrates — varies
children.appointment_method: Recruited and appointed by the Judicial Service Commission (Art. 172)
children.constitutional_basis: Art. 169, Constitution of Kenya 2010; Magistrates' Courts Act
children.notes: Also includes Kadhis' Courts: Chief Kadhi — Sheikh Abdulhalim Mohammed

children.appointment_method: Governors elected by popular vote per county (Art. 180)
children.notes: Devolved government — independent of the Executive; funded via equitable share from national revenue

members.notes: Replaced Kawira Mwangaza who was removed by County Assembly in 2024

children.current_officeholder: Speaker elected by MCAs in each County Assembly (47 Speakers total)
children.salary_source: SRC Gazette Notice No. 10350 (9 Aug 2023) — Speaker salary
children.appointment_method: Speaker elected by MCAs; MCAs elected by popular vote per ward (Art. 177)
children.notes: ~1,450+ ward seats + nominated MCAs for gender balance (one-third rule)

children.position: Members of County Assembly — MCAs (~1,450+ elected)
children.current_officeholder: Varies by ward — elected per ward every 5 years
children.appointment_method: Popular direct election — simple majority per ward (Art. 177); additional nominated seats for gender balance
children.notes: Full MCA lists maintained by each County Assembly — ~1,450 elected + ~500 nominated = ~1,950 total MCAs

children.position: County Executive Committee Members — CECMs (up to 10 per county)
children.current_officeholder: Varies by county — appointed by respective Governor, approved by County Assembly
children.appointment_method: Nominated by Governor; approved by County Assembly (Art. 179)
children.notes: Maximum 10 CECMs per county × 47 counties = up to 470 positions. Equivalent of Cabinet Secretaries at county level.

children.appointment_method: Various — established under Chapter 15, Constitution of Kenya 2010
children.notes: These offices are independent of all three arms of government

children.appointment_method: Nominated by President; approved by National Assembly (Art. 157)
children.notes: Replaced Noordin Haji who became NIS Director General

children.appointment_method: Nominated by President; approved by National Assembly (Art. 229)
Nominated by President; approved by National Assembly (Art. 228)
Selection Panel recommends; President appoints with National Assembly approval (IEBC Act)
children.notes: Reconstituted June 2025 after being without commissioners since February 2023 (Gazette Notices No. 7724 & 7725, 10 June 2025)

children.current_officeholder: Chair: Archbishop (Rtd) Eliud Wabukala; VC: Dr. Monica Wanjiru Muiru; CEO: Twalib Abdallah Mbarak
children.appointment_method: Nominated by President; approved by National Assembly (Art. 79)

children.constitutional_basis: Art. 230, Constitution of Kenya 2010; SRC Act, 2011
children.notes: Sets and reviews salaries of all State Officers
Recruits and disciplines national government civil servants

children.constitutional_basis: National Cohesion and Integration Act, 2008; Art. 248

children.notes: Civilian oversight of the National Police Service
Manages service matters (recruitment, discipline, promotions) for the National Police Service

children.constitutional_basis: Art. 59, Constitution of Kenya 2010; Commission on Administrative Justice Act
Art. 27; National Gender and Equality Commission Act

children.notes: Recommends equitable revenue sharing between national and county governments
